purpose: Replace the parallel direct-SQL catalog with canonical Medusa commerce records and query boundaries.
success_outcome: Catalog and product detail use Medusa-owned identity, prices, inventory, categories, and sales-channel visibility while preserving buyer behavior.

commands: npm --workspace apps/backend run test:integration -- catalog product-detail
success_checks: Seeded categories, products, variants, prices, inventory, sales-channel links, and publishable API key are Medusa records.
Catalog and product-detail routes resolve Medusa Query from the request scope and do not read eshop_local_catalog_* tables.
Responses preserve FT-001 and FT-002 contract behavior and expose stable Medusa variant IDs for later cart handoff.
evidence_required: Medusa workflow seed output with canonical record counts.
Integration output for catalog and product-detail mapping.
Proof that runtime query code does not reference parallel catalog tables.

stop_conditions: Canonical Medusa records cannot preserve required FT-001/FT-002 behavior.
Implementation requires a breaking storefront contract change.
Verification cannot prove stable Medusa variant identity.
required_handoff: Do not promote TASK-016 until canonical Medusa persistence and identity are verified.
